sections.paragraph:
συνοχή, ῆς, ἡ (s. συνέχω; Hom. et al.; LXX; EpArist 61; Jos., Ant. 8, 65)

① a place for confinement, prison (PLond II, 354, 24 p. 165 [10 b.c.]) ἐν ς. γενόμενος when he is put into prison D 1:5 (in the pl. bonds, fetters Manetho, Ap. 1, 313 al., several times in Vett. Val. index).

② a state of distress that involves a high degree of anxiety, distress, dismay, anguish (Artem. 2, 3 p. 88, 14; Astrampsychus p. 24 Dec. 42, 8; p. 26 Dec. 48, 10; BGU 1821, 21 and 28 [50 b.c.]; PLond I, 122, 35 p. 117 [IV a.d.]; Cat. Cod. Astr. VIII/1 p. 267, 5; Job 30:3; Ps. 24:17 Aq.) Lk 21:25. (W. θλῖψις) συνοχὴ καρδίας anguish of heart = troubled heart 2 Cor 2:4.—DELG s.v. 1 ἔχω 4. M-M. TW.

Hom Hom , VIII b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Jos. Jos. = Josephus. This abbr. used when follow by title; I a.d.—Lists 5

PLond PLond = PLondon=Greek Papyri in the British Museum—List 4

D D = Didache, except that in a list of manuscripts or as textual variant D refers to Codex Bezae—List 1

  1. pl. = plural

Manetho Manetho , hist., III b.c.—List 5

  1. al. =alibi (elsewhere), aliter (otherwise), alii (others)

Artem Artem , onir., II a.d.—List 5

Astrampsychus Astrampsychus , before IV b.c.; later works associated with his name, date uncertain—List 5

BGU BGU = Aegyptische Urkunden aus den Museen zu Berlin: Griechische Urkunden—List 4

Ps. Ps. = pseudo, pseudonymous

Aq Aq , II a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 974
